topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed with a notification, and a cold knot tightened in my stomach. It was a request. The name displayed on the screen made my breath catch. Mark. Mark Peterson. The name belonged to the shadow that had loomed over my life for the better part of a decade. My fingers hovered over the screen, slick with sudden sweat. What was he trying to do? A simple acknowledgment? Or something much worse? I quickly minimized the message, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. I stared at the blank screen, the silence of my room suddenly deafening, every shadow in the corner seeming to shift and coalesce into something menacing. I couldn't bring myself to look at the notification again.

[story 2]

The chime of a new connection startled me out of my reading. I blinked several times, trying to process the unexpected ping. It was Mark. The memory of him—the sneer, the way he used to corner me by the lockers—flashed vividly behind my eyes. A tremor ran through my hands as I picked up the device. My palms grew damp. A strange, unwelcome tension coiled deep in my chest, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I let the phone drop onto the desk, the small sound echoing loudly in the quiet apartment. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space, my gaze darting toward the closed door, waiting for something to happen.

[story 3]

When I saw the profile picture, a wave of nausea washed over me. It was him, undeniably, the same smirk etched onto his face from years ago. A distinct chill crept up my spine, settling in my bones. I gripped the edge of the table so tightly my knuckles turned white. This was impossible. He hadn't contacted me in years. My throat felt constricted, making it hard to form coherent thoughts. I swallowed hard, tasting metallic fear. I slowly reached for the notification, my movements jerky and hesitant, as if approaching a live wire. Every nerve ending seemed to be screaming a warning, urging me to run, but my feet remained rooted to the floor.
